title: goniopteris incisa
intro: Goniopteris incisa is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the cut-leaved scolopendrium or cut-leaved spleenwort. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Goniopteris incisa include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use in wound healing and as an antiseptic to prevent infection. In herbal preparations, Goniopteris incisa can be used in the form of a decoction, which is made by boiling the leaves in water, or as a poultice, which is applied directly to the affected area.
